item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm just glad the reality-altering SCP (oriented around physics) was originally a physics teacher instead of someone like me.
+1

item.title: Ideas for how SCP-4769 is encouraged to do various tests
item.content:encoded:
I have an idea for how the tests would have been conducted:

Maybe a syllabus with incorrect information or a textbook with incorrect information could be given to SCP-4769 for them to teach off of. Maybe that is how the various tests are conducted?

This could also be used to further disprove various incorrect theories like Newton's Corpuscle model of light, as it would break the universe in some way.

item.title: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded:
Noting that a recent reversion made by me was an error—I was informed that a set of edits made was unauthorized, but upon a closer look in the discussion permission was given. Thank you for noting this, author!

ETA: also, the reversion from Edna Granbo and edit lock breaking were also made in error and should not have happened. Sorry about the inconvenience.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
This is an original image taken by me. The person fully consented to taking the picture. I then took a picture of the picture and edited it to further obscure his face. He was fine with us using it if we did so.

I have included the image source in the information tab on the SCP page.
"
Image Source: Original Image taken by PolarPacificPolarPacific with consent given by individual in the image. (Purposely edited to obscure individuals identity)
"

item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
The image used in this article is currently unsourced, and therefore may be a violation of the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0). If you can provide the source for the image, then I will determine whether it is cc-compliant or not. If it is, then the image will stay. If it isn't, the image will go.

EDIT: The image has been sourced and is cc-compliant.

item.title: Liquid Hydrogen becomes a superfluid, not water.
item.content:encoded:
I'm pretty sure liquid hydrogen becomes superfluid near absolute zero, not water, since water would just freeze.

"Test 4: SCP-4769 is suggested to cool a cup of water down to absolute zero.
Result: Water flows through the bottom of the cup and through the ground."

Also maybe adding how he was suggested into doing so would be great.

item.title: Letting someone else edit
item.content:encoded: Im not really sure about the protocol surrounding this but I give permission to Helioskrub to edit this SCP page.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like the concept of this SCP; but at the same time, Document 4769-Beta feels, incongruous with what it does? I'm not sure if it's just the way it's phrased or what, but there doesn't feel like there's a real connection between the "SCP 4769 lectures on physics but gets stuff wrong which alters reality to fit" and the experiments where strange stuff is suggested to it and it creates experiments which does it? I feel like adding a little more context to this would make the connection feel better?

The other thing, which isn't necessarily an issue, is that you mention "SCP-4769 appears to have lower than average intelligence" but this feels, somewhat inconsistent with the way it lectures on physics which seems largely accurate but with slight mistakes?

…I know this post sounds really critical here but I do actually really like this as a concept even if I think it needs a little more work to really live up to it's potential
